building_row.open_questions: Because the building is new and review-light, verify real-world sound transfer, rooftop/social-lounge noise, package handling, parking assignment availability, and after-hours support. Confirm exact pet charges, concession math, and whether the shown free parking language is still accurate for the target lease.
building_row.notes: The Baxter is a strong paper fit: washer/dryer, dishwasher, AC, secured parcel room, EV charging, rooftop communal work space, bike storage, pool, gym, and pet-friendly policy are all publicly visible. The main problem is evidence depth, not clear failure. Apartments.com shows a very high 4.9/5 blended score, but only two renter reviews are counted and the visible review section is still effectively thin. Quiet risk is not negligible because the building is a short walk from Hollywood/Highland and includes rooftop social amenities, but being on Cherokee rather than directly on Hollywood Boulevard may help. Pricing transparency is only moderate because concessions are public and parking is listed as free on Apartments.com, yet the same page still tells prospects to call for parking-policy details and the official site does not publish a simple fee table.

evidence_rows.claim: The official site describes The Baxter as an 86-unit, seven-story building with a rooftop deck, social lounge, and onsite parking.
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: The official page says the building pairs its design with modern amenities such as a rooftop deck, social lounge, and onsite parking.

evidence_rows.claim: Apartments.com lists package service, secured parcel room, EV charging, pool, gym, dog park, bike storage, communal working space, and in-unit washer/dryer, AC, and dishwasher.
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: Visible amenities include Package Service, EV Charging, Pool, Fitness Center, Dog Park, Bicycle Storage, Coffee Bar in Lobby, Communal Working space, Secured Parcel Room, Washer/Dryer, Air Conditioning, and Dishwasher.

evidence_rows.claim: Resident-review signal is currently too thin to treat the high headline rating as durable proof of operations.
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com shows a 4.9/5 blended score with 2 renter reviews, but the visible review section is still very light. That supports interest, not confidence.

evidence_rows.claim: Parking and concession disclosures are directionally helpful but still not fully clean or self-contained.
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com says parking is available and free of charge for residents, lists garage parking at $0/mo, and notes 1 Month Free, but also says to call for more information on parking policy and fees.

evidence_rows.claim: The Baxter is close to core Hollywood daily needs and transit, but that same proximity raises daily street-noise exposure.
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com says Hollywood/Highland Station is 0.4 mile away and Musicians Institute is 0.3 mile away, which is great for routine access but places the building in an active Hollywood pocket.

evidence_rows.claim: The building is recently delivered, so long-run operational stability and renewal behavior are still unproven.
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: Urbanize reported in July 2024 that construction was complete and described an 86-unit building above a two-level, 61-car basement garage. That recency explains why public resident history is still thin.

packet_markdown:
# The Baxter building packet

## Snapshot
- Building ID: the-baxter-1818-n-cherokee-ave
- Address: 1818 N Cherokee Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90028
- Primary URL: https://thebaxterhollywood.com/
- Management company: SD Property Management
- Recommended status: tour_candidate
- Last updated: 2026-03-25

## Scorecard
- Quiet: 3 (low, evidence count 2)
- Management: 3 (low, evidence count 3)
- Amenity: 5 (high, evidence count 4)
- Community stability: 3 (low, evidence count 2)
- Location fit: 5
- Pricing transparency: 3

## Risks
- Structural noise risk: medium
- Location noise risk: high
- Security risk: low
- Pricing risk: medium

## Evidence summary
- [official] The official site describes The Baxter as an 86-unit, seven-story building with a rooftop deck, social lounge, and onsite parking. Source: The Baxter official site (https://thebaxterhollywood.com/). Note: The official page says the building pairs its design with modern amenities such as a rooftop deck, social lounge, and onsite parking.
- [review] Apartments.com lists package service, secured parcel room, EV charging, pool, gym, dog park, bike storage, communal working space, and in-unit washer/dryer, AC, and dishwasher. Source: Apartments.com (https://www.apartments.com/the-baxter-los-angeles-ca/mtsg17j/). Note: Visible amenities include Package Service, EV Charging, Pool, Fitness Center, Dog Park, Bicycle Storage, Coffee Bar in Lobby, Communal Working space, Secured Parcel Room, Washer/Dryer, Air Conditioning, and Dishwasher.
- [review] Resident-review signal is currently too thin to treat the high headline rating as durable proof of operations. Source: Apartments.com (https://www.apartments.com/the-baxter-los-angeles-ca/mtsg17j/). Note: Apartments.com shows a 4.9/5 blended score with 2 renter reviews, but the visible review section is still very light. That supports interest, not confidence.
- [review] Parking and concession disclosures are directionally helpful but still not fully clean or self-contained. Source: Apartments.com (https://www.apartments.com/the-baxter-los-angeles-ca/mtsg17j/). Note: Apartments.com says parking is available and free of charge for residents, lists garage parking at $0/mo, and notes 1 Month Free, but also says to call for more information on parking policy and fees.
- [review] The Baxter is close to core Hollywood daily needs and transit, but that same proximity raises daily street-noise exposure. Source: Apartments.com location section (https://www.apartments.com/the-baxter-los-angeles-ca/mtsg17j/). Note: Apartments.com says Hollywood/Highland Station is 0.4 mile away and Musicians Institute is 0.3 mile away, which is great for routine access but places the building in an active Hollywood pocket.
- [news] The building is recently delivered, so long-run operational stability and renewal behavior are still unproven. Source: Urbanize LA (https://la.urbanize.city/post/rendering-vs-reality-baxter-hollywood-apartments-1818-n-cherokee-avenue). Note: Urbanize reported in July 2024 that construction was complete and described an 86-unit building above a two-level, 61-car basement garage. That recency explains why public resident history is still thin.

## Open questions
- Verify real-world sound transfer and rooftop/social-lounge noise in the exact stack.
- Confirm package-handling reliability, parking assignment availability, and after-hours human support.
- Confirm exact pet charges, concession math, and whether the free-parking language still applies to the target lease.

## Notes
- Very strong paper amenity fit for a new Hollywood building.
- The limiting factor is operating history: the building is still too new and review-light for high-confidence management or quiet scores.
- Worth touring, but only with a skeptical operations checklist.
